text.title: arch-pattern-clean Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for arch-pattern-clean as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Putting business logic in controllers (fat controller).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Controllers are in the Presentation ring; business logic belongs in Use Cases or Entities so it can be reused by CLI, queue workers, and scheduled jobs without HTTP overhead.

failure-mode.description: Importing ORM models directly from the domain layer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ORMs are an infrastructure detail. Domain must depend on nothing application-level — no logging, no ORM, no clock. Inject everything via ports.

failure-mode.description: Exposing domain entities via the API (JSON-serialising them directly).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Domain entities change on business rules; API contracts change on consumer needs. Explicit DTOs at adapter boundaries prevent API breakage when the domain evolves.

failure-mode.description: Skipping repository interfaces and calling the ORM directly from use cases.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use cases become untestable without a real database, and swapping persistence requires touching business logic.
